ipoTracker.status: October 2026 Nasdaq IPO target; Samsung chip talks signal hardware ambitions; ARR $47B; Fable 5 Bug Bounty day 2; Pentagon dispute ongoing; October Nasdaq target intact
ipoTracker.process: Anthropic confirmed its confidential draft Form S-1 submission to the SEC on June 1, 2026, targeting an October 2026 Nasdaq IPO led by Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, and Morgan Stanley with an expected raise of $60B+. The $65B Series H closed at a $965B post-money valuation, led by Altimeter Capital, Dragoneer, Greenoaks, and Sequoia Capital, with $15B from hyperscalers including $5B from Amazon, plus Micron, Samsung, and SK hynix. ARR: $47B as of May 2026. Fable 5 Bug Bounty on HackerOne (launched July 4) is in day 2 — the first public AI vulnerability disclosure program for a frontier model. Early-stage Samsung chip talks (July 2 report) signal Anthropic's intent to develop in-house silicon, a new strategic dimension ahead of the IPO.
ipoTracker.note: Samsung chip talks are early-stage but represent a significant pre-IPO signal that Anthropic is building toward silicon independence alongside AWS Trainium, Google TPU, and NVIDIA. October 2026 Nasdaq target remains the working assumption.

latestArticle.title: Anthropic in Talks With Samsung to Manufacture Custom AI Chip

ipoTracker.status: IPO leaning 2027; 5% government equity stake proposal conceptual and may require act of Congress; GPT-5.6 broader access expected July 10-17; 42-state AG probe ongoing; ChatGPT market share 46.4%
ipoTracker.process: The previous status regarding the confidential S-1 filing, 2027 IPO lean, and the conceptual government stake remains in place. New reports suggest OpenAI is pivoting toward a 'superapp' model (Item 2) and that industry observers are noting the current favorable timing for an IPO (Item 3).
ipoTracker.confidence: Media speculation; previous status confirmed by company self-disclosure
ipoTracker.note: The core details regarding the S-1, government stake, and timeline have not been contradicted or updated by these articles.

latestArticle.title: OpenAI readies ‘superapp’ pivot ahead of planned IPO, FT reports - Fortune

ipoTracker.status: SPCX ~$157.54 (July 3 close; markets closed July 4-5); Grok 4.5 private beta confirmed; 2T model late July; Grok 5 in development; both Colossus committed; $60B Cursor Q3 close on track
ipoTracker.process: SpaceX priced at $135/share on June 11, raising $75 billion at a $1.75 trillion valuation in the largest IPO ever recorded. Shares peaked at $225.64 on June 16 and are at $157.54 as of July 3. Reflection AI's $150M/month Colossus 2 deal started July 1 — both Colossus facilities are now committed to third parties. Grok 4.5 (1.5 trillion parameters, V9 architecture) is in private beta at Tesla and SpaceX. A 2T parameter successor is in final training expected late July. Grok 5 (6-10T parameters) is in development on Colossus 2. The $60B Cursor acquisition is on track for Q3 2026 close.
ipoTracker.confidence: Confirmed; public company as of June 12, 2026
ipoTracker.note: Grok 5 development on Colossus 2 is a new strategic signal. Cursor Q3 close remains the next major positive catalyst. Both Colossus facilities commercialized to third parties raises questions about xAI's own dedicated training compute.

latestArticle.title: Grok 4.5 private beta confirmed at SpaceX and Tesla; Grok 5 development begins

ipoTracker.status: CBRS public (Nasdaq, May 2026); Q1 2026 revenue $193.4M (+92% YoY); GPT-5.6 Sol Cerebras deployment expected this month at 750 tok/sec; $20B+ OpenAI compute deal active
ipoTracker.process: Cerebras completed its Nasdaq IPO in May 2026 under ticker CBRS. Q1 2026 results (released June 23) massively beat estimates: $193.4M revenue vs. $56.65M expected (+92% YoY). Major deals: multi-year OpenAI compute deal (750MW, $20B+ valued, option to $30B) and a multi-year AWS partnership pairing Cerebras CS-3 with Trainium 3 for inference disaggregation. OpenAI is deploying GPT-5.6 Sol on Cerebras wafer-scale hardware this month at up to 750 tokens per second.
ipoTracker.note: GPT-5.6 Sol Cerebras deployment this month is the key near-term positive catalyst. Post-earnings margin guidance selloff remains the near-term stock risk to watch.

latestArticle.title: Cerebras Systems Announces Strong First Quarter 2026 Results

latestArticle.title: RVI Fund Eyes Best Week Ever — Retail Chases Databricks Pre-IPO Exposure - Stocktwits

ipoTracker.stage: Watchlist candidate — no primary-source filing confirmed
ipoTracker.status: Unverified filing claims; no SEC or company confirmation found; $14B+ raised June 2025
ipoTracker.process: Some IPO tracker sites claim Scale AI filed an S-1 in March 2026, but no matching SEC filing, Scale AI announcement, or credible primary-source confirmation has been found as of July 5, 2026. Scale raised more than $14 billion in June 2025 and is unlikely to pursue a near-term IPO given its recent large private financing.
ipoTracker.confidence: Low until verified by primary source
ipoTracker.note: No change from prior checks. No SEC EDGAR record for Scale AI found. Tracker-only claims remain unconfirmed.

latestArticle.title: Can You Invest in Scale AI Pre-IPO?

ipoTracker.stage: High-growth private company — 2028 IPO target confirmed; Apple acquisition talks unresolved
ipoTracker.status: 2028 IPO reaffirmed; Windows PC targeting this month; Perplexity for Gov launched; $500M ARR; 100M MAU; $20B valuation; Apple reportedly discussed acquisition at $20B
ipoTracker.process: CEO Aravind Srinivas publicly stated a preference for an independent 2028 IPO. ARR reached $500 million (+335% YoY) with 100M+ monthly active users and a $20 billion valuation from the May 2026 round. Personal Computer GA on Mac since May 7; Windows version with hybrid local-cloud inference orchestrator targeting July 2026. Perplexity for Gov launched alongside the GSA with FedRAMP compliance underway. Apple executives have reportedly discussed acquiring Perplexity at its $20B valuation — no move has been made.
ipoTracker.confidence: No filing; 2028 IPO is stated preference; Apple acquisition reported but not confirmed
ipoTracker.note: Windows Personal Computer this month and potential Apple acquisition interest are the two key July catalysts. 2028 IPO timeline is firm per CEO.

latestArticle.title: Apple reportedly discussed acquiring Perplexity AI

ipoTracker.status: IPO at $42/share; raised $2.1B; $15B market cap; ticker HFCE on Nasdaq; open-source AI infrastructure bellwether
ipoTracker.process: Hugging Face completed its Nasdaq IPO on June 9, 2026, pricing at $42 per share and raising $2.1 billion at a $15 billion market cap under ticker HFCE. The IPO is widely seen as an open-source AI watershed — the first major public listing of a company whose primary assets are open-source model repositories, training infrastructure, and developer community.
ipoTracker.confidence: Confirmed; public company as of June 9, 2026
ipoTracker.note: HFCE is a publicly traded open-source AI infrastructure bellwether. Track as an indicator of investor appetite for open-source AI business models.

latestArticle.title: Inside the Hugging Face IPO: What It Means for Open-Source AI in 2026

partnershipTracker.type: Cloud, product distribution, and IP licensing
partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed amended partnership; Copilot Cowork billing day 5; Frontier Co. deepens enterprise channel; GPT-5.6 restricted; broader access expected July 10-17
partnershipTracker.terms: Microsoft remains OpenAI's primary cloud partner under a non-exclusive IP license through 2032 (amended April 27, 2026). GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna remain restricted to approximately 20 organizations; broader access expected July 10-17. Copilot Cowork billing at $0.01/Copilot Credit is in its fifth day. Microsoft Frontier Co. ($2.5B, 6,000 embedded engineers) adds enterprise professional services deployment capacity for OpenAI products. Microsoft is evaluating DeepSeek V4 for some Cowork workloads to reduce API costs.
partnershipTracker.note: Frontier Co. adds enterprise deployment muscle for OpenAI products. July 10-17 GPT-5.6 broader access is the next model availability milestone for this relationship.

partnershipTracker.type: GPU infrastructure and strategic supplier relationship
partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed; 10GW partnership; H2 2026 first-gigawatt Vera Rubin deployment on track; Vera Rubin production shipments to hyperscalers began July 2026
partnershipTracker.terms: NVIDIA agreed to deploy at least 10GW of NVIDIA systems for OpenAI infrastructure, with the first gigawatt targeted for H2 2026 on Vera Rubin. NVIDIA invested $30 billion in Stargate in March 2026. Vera Rubin production shipments began in July 2026 to AWS, Google Cloud, Microsoft Azure, OCI, and CoreWeave — the infrastructure backbone that will host OpenAI's H2 2026 compute expansion.
partnershipTracker.note: Vera Rubin shipments beginning in July 2026 to hyperscalers advances the H2 2026 first-gigawatt milestone. No structural change to the relationship on July 5.

latestArticle.title: OpenAI and NVIDIA Announce Strategic Partnership to Deploy 10 Gigawatts of NVIDIA Systems

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed; 6GW partnership beginning with 1GW MI450 in H2 2026; MI450 samples shipping to OpenAI; Oracle 50K unit MI450 cluster adds adjacent capacity; AMD Advancing AI 2026 July 22-23
partnershipTracker.terms: OpenAI and AMD agreed on a multi-generation 6GW GPU deployment beginning with an initial MI450 rollout of approximately 1GW in H2 2026. AMD CEO Lisa Su confirmed MI450 samples are shipping to OpenAI. Oracle's 50,000-unit MI450 deployment in Q3 2026 adds the first publicly available MI450-based AI supercluster, providing an adjacent cloud resource for OpenAI workloads. AMD Advancing AI 2026 (July 22-23) is expected to formally launch MI450.
partnershipTracker.note: AMD Advancing AI 2026 on July 22-23 is the next major milestone for this relationship — formal MI450 launch and Helios rack confirmation expected.

latestArticle.title: AMD and OpenAI announce strategic partnership to deploy 6 gigawatts of AMD GPUs

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed; Stargate Abilene TX flagship (1.2 GW, Oracle Cloud) fully operational; $50B Amazon and $30B NVIDIA Stargate investments committed; Jalapeño ASIC targeting H2 2026 Stargate deployment
partnershipTracker.terms: OpenAI's Stargate compute platform spans Oracle, SoftBank, CoreWeave, and related infrastructure partners. The Abilene, TX flagship (1.2 GW on Oracle Cloud) is fully operational. Amazon invested $50 billion and NVIDIA invested $30 billion in the March 2026 Stargate funding round. Jalapeño — OpenAI's first custom ASIC co-developed with Broadcom — targets H2 2026 Stargate deployment with 1.3 GW compute committed for 2027.
partnershipTracker.note: Jalapeño H2 2026 Stargate deployment is the next major structural milestone for this partnership. Treat Stargate as a living infrastructure network rather than a static venture.

latestArticle.title: OpenAI and Broadcom unveil Jalapeño — first custom AI inference chip

partnershipTracker.status: Meta's announcement of Meta Compute cloud is causing negative market sentiment regarding CoreWeave's backlog defense.
partnershipTracker.terms: The previous agreement established a $21B take-or-pay commitment through December 2032. However, recent news indicates that Meta's own compute cloud announcements are weighing on investor concerns about CoreWeave's ability to defend its existing backlog.
partnershipTracker.note: The articles focus on market reaction rather than contract terms.

latestArticle.title: CoreWeave (NASDAQ:CRWV) falls as Nasdaq-100 spot faces pressure, Meta cloud worries weigh on AI pricing - TechStock²

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed; multi-year agreement; Vera Rubin NVL72 clusters at full utilization following Fable 5 restoration July 1
partnershipTracker.terms: CoreWeave and Anthropic entered a multi-year agreement for Claude workloads at production scale, with infrastructure rolling out in phases beginning in 2026. Financial terms were not disclosed. Following the July 1 Fable 5 restoration, full Anthropic Claude inference traffic is running on CoreWeave's Vera Rubin NVL72 clusters. CoreWeave's first Vera Rubin NVL72 production validation delivered 4x training and 10x inference-per-watt improvements over Blackwell.
partnershipTracker.note: Fable 5 restoration July 1 restored full utilization of CoreWeave's Vera Rubin NVL72 clusters for Anthropic. No new contract terms disclosed on July 5.

latestArticle.title: CoreWeave Announces Multi-Year Agreement With Anthropic

partnershipTracker.status: Rebuilding after Fable 5 ban; full Claude restored July 1; Pentagon dispute court docs (released July 4) reveal unresolved policy disagreement over autonomous weapons; Maven Smart System retains DoD program-of-record designation
partnershipTracker.terms: Palantir and Anthropic bring Claude to U.S. government intelligence and defense users inside Palantir products running on AWS infrastructure. The Fable 5 ban (June 12-July 1) disrupted the pipeline, with the Pentagon reportedly migrating two-thirds of its AI workflows off Anthropic models. Court documents released July 4 reveal a dispute between Dario Amodei and Pentagon Undersecretary Emil Michael over autonomous weapons and domestic surveillance limits. Full Fable 5 access was restored July 1. Maven Smart System retains its DoD program-of-record designation.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed partnership; Pentagon dispute details from court documents reported July 4
partnershipTracker.note: Pentagon dispute court docs are a new risk factor for the defense pipeline. The underlying policy disagreement over autonomous weapons access has not been resolved. The partnership is rebuilding after the 19-day Fable 5 ban.

latestArticle.title: Pentagon contract dispute documents released — Amodei vs. Emil Michael

partnershipTracker.type: AI model integration and distribution agreement
partnershipTracker.status: Strained; Apple's reported consideration of Mistral AI and Perplexity suggests continued diversification away from OpenAI.
partnershipTracker.terms: Apple and OpenAI previously had an integration agreement for ChatGPT in iOS/Siri, but this has become strained. While the previous status noted Apple evaluating Mistral and discussing acquiring Perplexity, the latest news confirms a potential legal battle between the two companies.

latestArticle.title: Apple-OpenAI Partnership Frays, Setting Up A Possible Legal Battle Meteo Varese (iAzOoMbXHB) - Mshale

partnershipTracker.type: GPU supply and cloud AI infrastructure partnership
partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed ongoing supplier relationship; MI300X powers Azure AI workloads; MI450 evaluation for Azure underway; Vera Rubin production to Azure begins July 2026 (NVIDIA primary)
partnershipTracker.terms: AMD is a GPU supplier to Microsoft Azure for AI workloads, with MI300X and MI450 (when available) powering Azure AI compute capacity alongside NVIDIA hardware. Microsoft is evaluating MI450 for Azure data center deployments in H2 2026. NVIDIA remains Microsoft's primary GPU partner for new Azure AI capacity through the Anthropic-Microsoft-NVIDIA strategic alliance.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed ongoing relationship; MI450 Azure evaluation reported but not announced
partnershipTracker.note: AMD Advancing AI 2026 (July 22-23) may clarify Azure MI450 deployment terms. This remains a supplier-customer relationship rather than a formal strategic co-investment.

latestArticle.title: AMD Advancing AI 2026 — July 22-23 at Moscone Center

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed; $60B/6GW multi-generation agreement remains in place, but Meta is reportedly considering a major chip order from Samsung.
partnershipTracker.terms: AMD and Meta maintain their existing $60 billion, 6GW multi-generation GPU supply agreement. While the core AMD relationship continues, new reports suggest Meta could award Samsung its largest AI chip order yet in a potential $6.5 billion deal.
partnershipTracker.note: The previous MI450 Helios rack H2 2026 milestone is unaffected by these news items.

latestArticle.title: Meta Could Hand Samsung Its Biggest AI Chip Order Yet in $6.5 Billion Deal - Yahoo Finance

partnershipTracker.type: Strategic equity investment and primary GPU supply relationship
partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed; NVIDIA holds ~11% equity stake; CoreWeave is primary commercial Vera Rubin deployer; revenue-sharing model expands relationship beyond hardware procurement
partnershipTracker.terms: NVIDIA holds approximately 11% equity stake in CoreWeave (approximately $2 billion in value). CoreWeave purchases NVIDIA hardware at scale — $31 billion capex floor for 2026 alone — and is among the first cloud providers to deploy Vera Rubin NVL72 racks in production. NVIDIA's new revenue-sharing AI factory model (announced July 2) includes CoreWeave as a deployer alongside Sharon AI and Firmus, potentially deepening the economic alignment beyond one-time hardware sales. Vera Rubin production shipments to CoreWeave began in July 2026.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed; equity stake and supply relationship confirmed by both companies
partnershipTracker.note: NVIDIA's revenue-sharing model announced July 2 is a new dimension of this relationship — CoreWeave may participate as a revenue-sharing AI factory partner rather than purely a hardware purchaser. This could improve CoreWeave economics at scale.

latestArticle.title: NVIDIA unveils revenue-sharing AI factory model — Sharon AI first deployer with 40,000 GB300 GPUs
